perturbation.original_text: For a period of four years from the date of this Agreement (the "Restricted Period"), Seller Parties shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in The Company's Business (as hereinafter defined) or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, own an interest in, manage, operate, join, control, lend money or render financial or other assistance to or participate in, as a partner, stockholder, consultant or otherwise, any Person that competes with The Company's Business as it is conducted as of the Closing Date.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, all of which together shall constitute one and the same Agreement. For a period of four years from the date of this Agreement (the "Restricted Period"), Seller Parties shall not, directly or indirectly, engage in The Company's Business (as hereinafter defined) or, without the prior written consent of Buyer Parties, directly or indirectly, own an interest in, manage, operate, join, control, lend money or render financial or other assistance to or participate in, as a partner, stockholder, consultant or otherwise, any Person that competes with The Company's Business as it is conducted as of the Closing Date.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the non-compete clause to a section that deals with administrative matters (counterparts) obscures the importance and enforceability of the clause. Non-compete agreements are usually expected to be clearly delineated and brought to the attention of the signing party. By placing it under the counterparts section, enforceability will be reduced.
perturbation.law_explanation: Given that Simtek Corporation is a Colorado Corporation, this agreement is related to Colorado. Colorado law has specific requirements for non-compete agreements to be enforceable. While not directly contradicting the statute's explicit wording about reasonableness, burying it in a miscellaneous section like 'Counterparts' makes it harder to argue that the employee had clear notice and understood the implications, especially regarding trade secrets, which directly impacts its enforceability under Colorado law. Placement affects enforceability, as notice is a factor considered.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation and law explanation suggest that moving the non-compete clause to the 'Counterparts' section weakens its enforceability due to reduced notice and potential lack of clear understanding by the signing party, especially regarding trade secrets. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction,' where a required legal disclosure is placed in a misleading section. While there is no specific law snippet to point to, the explanation provided indicates that the placement of the clause affects its enforceability under Colorado law due to notice requirements, suggesting a significant contradiction.
